name: Security Review
description: Scans source files for common security anti-patterns and dangerous API usage

checks.fail: ❌ No package.json — cannot perform dependency analysis

checks.pass: ✅ No eval() usage found in src/
checks.fail: 🔴 eval() detected — arbitrary code execution risk
failRecommendations: Replace eval() with safer alternatives (JSON.parse, Function constructors are also dangerous)
Audit all dynamic code execution paths
Sanitize all HTML content before rendering
Use DOMPurify or a similar library
Replace with safe text interpolation wherever possible

checks.pass: ✅ No hardcoded credentials detected in src/
failRecommendations: Move all secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager
Rotate any secrets that were committed
Add a pre-commit hook (e.g. gitleaks) to prevent future leaks

checks.pass: ✅ No direct child_process usage in src/ (or acceptable usage found)
checks.fail: ⚠️  child_process imported in source files — review for command injection
failRecommendations: Sanitize all user-supplied data before passing to exec/spawn
Prefer execFile over exec to avoid shell injection

checks.prompt: Review the package.json dependencies and devDependencies for packages with known security vulnerabilities, deprecated packages, or packages commonly associated with supply-chain attacks. Flag any findings as CRITICAL if they have active CVEs or are known malicious packages.
